stack: Laravel 12 + Inertia.js v2 + React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4
summary: Production-grade SaaS growth stack with strong foundations across SEO, onboarding, monetization, and retention. Major gaps in team/org features, referral system, PWA support, and A/B testing infrastructure limit expansion revenue potential.

seo_and_discoverability.summary: Comprehensive SEO foundation with SSR, structured data, sitemap, robots.txt, llms.txt, canonical URLs, and auth-aware indexing. Minor gaps in Web Vitals monitoring and image optimization.

strengths.title: Full SSR via Inertia server-side rendering
strengths.detail: resources/js/ssr.tsx configured with ReactDOMServer, vite.config.ts SSR build, ensuring crawlers see fully-rendered HTML.

strengths.title: Comprehensive structured data (JSON-LD)
strengths.detail: SoftwareApplication, Organization, FAQPage, BreadcrumbList, BlogPosting, and Article schemas implemented across pages. BreadcrumbJsonLd.tsx and FaqJsonLd.tsx reusable components.

strengths.title: Dynamic sitemap with caching
strengths.detail: SeoController generates sitemap.xml with priority levels, change frequencies, and dynamic blog post entries. 24-hour cache TTL.

strengths.title: Auth-aware robot directives
strengths.detail: app.blade.php serves noindex/nofollow for authenticated users, index/follow with rich snippet directives for public visitors. Prevents dashboard/profile indexing.

strengths.title: llms.txt AI training directive
strengths.detail: SeoController serves /llms.txt per llmstxt.org specification listing authorized/unauthorized content for AI crawlers.

strengths.title: Per-page meta tags and OpenGraph
strengths.detail: All marketing pages set custom og:title, og:description, og:image, Twitter cards. Default OG image at public/images/og-default.png.

strengths.title: Semantic slug-based URL structure
strengths.detail: /blog/{slug}, /features/{feature}, /guides/{guide}, /compare/{competitor} — all named routes via Ziggy.

findings.title: No Web Vitals monitoring
findings.detail: No web-vitals npm package integrated. Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) not tracked or sent to GA4.
findings.recommendation: Install web-vitals package and forward metrics to GA4 via custom events.

findings.title: Missing image optimization metadata
findings.detail: No width/height attributes on all images, no WebP/AVIF format variants, no responsive srcset patterns.
findings.recommendation: Add width/height to prevent CLS, implement responsive images with srcset for marketing pages.

findings.title: No structured pricing schema
findings.detail: Pricing page lacks Offer or AggregateOffer schema markup.
findings.recommendation: Add AggregateOffer JSON-LD to /pricing page for rich results.

findings.title: No trailing slash policy enforcement
findings.detail: No middleware or redirect to enforce /page vs /page/ consistency. Could cause duplicate content.
findings.recommendation: Add middleware to canonicalize URL trailing slashes.

findings.title: Single sitemap without index
findings.detail: Works for current ~100 URLs but won't scale. No sitemap_index.xml for splitting by content type.
findings.recommendation: Implement sitemap index when URL count exceeds 500.

onboarding_and_activation.summary: Exceptional onboarding with 3-step wizard, lifecycle tracking, health scoring, engagement scoring, welcome email sequences, and session data migration. Minor gaps in guided tours and progressive disclosure.

strengths.title: 3-step onboarding wizard with use-case capture
strengths.detail: OnboardingController + Onboarding.tsx: Welcome → Preferences → Get Started. Captures use_case (saas_founder, agency, internal_tool, evaluating), timezone, theme. Context-aware CTAs based on use case and feature flags.

strengths.title: 7-stage lifecycle management
strengths.detail: LifecycleService tracks VISITOR → TRIAL → ACTIVATED → PAYING → EXPANSION / AT_RISK → CHURNED with transition history, reason tracking, and days-in-stage metadata.

strengths.title: Dual health + engagement scoring
strengths.detail: CustomerHealthService (login frequency, feature adoption, billing status, profile completion) and EngagementScoringService (login recency, features, onboarding, account age) both 0-100 with batch optimization.

strengths.title: Welcome email sequence (3 emails)
strengths.detail: WelcomeSequenceNotification: Welcome aboard → 3 things to try → Advanced features. Feature-aware content, checks enabled features before mentioning.

strengths.title: Lead scoring with MQL/SQL thresholds
strengths.detail: LeadScoringService: composite formula (engagement*0.5 + health*0.3 + pql_signal*0.2). MQL at 60, SQL at 80. QualifyLeads command runs batched scoring.

strengths.title: Onboarding middleware guard
strengths.detail: EnsureOnboardingCompleted middleware redirects incomplete users. Feature-gated, checks user_settings.onboarding_completed timestamp.

strengths.title: Session data migration service
strengths.detail: SessionDataMigrationService migrates guest session data on registration. Pattern documented and integrated into registration flow.

findings.title: No guided feature tours
findings.detail: No step-by-step feature discovery system (Shepherd.js, Intro.js). Only basic info-tooltips exist. First-time users don't get contextual guidance on dashboard features.
findings.recommendation: Implement lightweight feature tour for first dashboard visit using a tour library or custom step-through component.

findings.title: No progressive feature disclosure
findings.detail: Features don't unlock progressively based on milestones. All enabled features are visible immediately regardless of adoption level.
findings.recommendation: Consider revealing advanced features (webhooks, API tokens) only after basic setup completion.

findings.title: No automated NPS trigger post-onboarding
findings.detail: NpsSurveyController exists with eligibility logic but no automatic trigger after onboarding completion.
findings.recommendation: Trigger NPS eligibility check 7 days after onboarding_completed timestamp.

findings.title: No video onboarding or interactive demos
findings.detail: No embedded video guides, animated GIFs, or interactive product demos in the onboarding flow.
findings.recommendation: Add optional video embed to onboarding step 1 for visual learners.

conversion_and_monetization.summary: Production-grade billing with Stripe Cashier, 5-tier pricing, trial management, dunning recovery, retention coupons, and annual/monthly toggle. Gaps in abandoned checkout recovery, churn reason analytics, and LTV/CAC tracking.

strengths.title: 5-tier pricing with per-seat billing
strengths.detail: Free → Pro ($19/mo) → Pro Team ($39/seat/mo) → Team ($49/seat/mo) → Enterprise. Annual 20% discount. A/B variant pricing for Pro tier. Coming-soon soft-launch mode.

strengths.title: Redis-locked subscription mutations
strengths.detail: BillingService wraps all Stripe operations in Redis locks (35s timeout) + DB transactions. Prevents concurrent race conditions on create, swap, cancel, resume, quantity update.

strengths.title: Comprehensive dunning with 3-stage escalation
strengths.detail: DunningReminderNotification: gentle → urgency → final notice. PaymentActionRequiredNotification for 3DS. PaymentRecoveredNotification on success. InvoluntaryChurnWinBackNotification 3 days post-cancel. Grace period configurable (7 days default).

strengths.title: Retention coupon on cancellation
strengths.detail: CancelSubscriptionDialog offers 20% off 3 months (configurable RETENTION_COUPON_ID) when user selects cancellation reason. Applied via applyRetentionCoupon() endpoint.

strengths.title: Trial-to-paid conversion pipeline
strengths.detail: 14-day configurable trial granting Pro tier access. Urgency alerts (color-coded: info/warning/urgent). Trial expiration cleanup. TRIAL_CONVERTED analytics event. CheckExpiredTrials command.

strengths.title: PQL-based upgrade prompts
strengths.detail: UpgradePrompt.tsx triggers at 50%, 80%, 100% plan limit thresholds. PQL_THRESHOLD events feed into lead scoring. Next-tier calculation automatic.

findings.title: No abandoned checkout recovery
findings.detail: No mechanism to detect and recover abandoned Stripe Checkout sessions. Users who start checkout but don't complete are lost without follow-up.
findings.recommendation: Listen to checkout.session.expired Stripe webhook and send recovery email with direct checkout link.

findings.title: No LTV/CAC calculation
findings.detail: AdminBillingStatsService tracks MRR, churn rate, trial conversion but not Customer Lifetime Value or Customer Acquisition Cost.
findings.recommendation: Calculate LTV = (ARPU * gross_margin) / churn_rate. Track CAC by acquisition_channel from UTM data.

findings.title: No churn reason analytics
findings.detail: Cancellation dialog collects reason/feedback but no aggregation, trending, or admin dashboard for churn reasons.
findings.recommendation: Add admin view aggregating cancellation reasons with trend charts. Feed into product decisions.

findings.title: No downgrade friction or feature-loss warnings
findings.detail: Plan swaps execute immediately without warning users about features they'll lose. No data migration or grace period for downgrade.
findings.recommendation: Show feature comparison diff on downgrade. Warn about data that may become inaccessible (e.g., tokens over new limit).

findings.title: No expansion revenue tracking
findings.detail: Seat upgrades and plan swaps logged but no aggregate 'expansion MRR' metric or net revenue retention calculation.
findings.recommendation: Add expansion MRR = sum of upgrade revenue - contraction revenue to AdminBillingStatsService.

findings.title: No invoice customization
findings.detail: Invoices use default Cashier/DomPDF template. No company logo, custom footer, or tax ID display.
findings.recommendation: Configure Cashier invoice renderer with branding in config/cashier.php.

retention_and_engagement.summary: Enterprise-grade retention system with 21 notification classes, 4-email re-engagement sequences, 3-email win-back campaigns, lifecycle automation, health/engagement scoring, and multi-channel delivery. NPS and feedback loops integrated.

strengths.title: 21 notification classes with dual-channel delivery
strengths.detail: Email + database (in-app) channels for all campaigns. HasUnsubscribeLink trait. Conditional routing based on email verification status.

strengths.title: 4-email re-engagement sequence
strengths.detail: SendReEngagementEmails command: day 7 gentle check-in, day 14 feedback request, day 21 account status, day 30 value tip. A/B variant for high-engagement users (score >= 60). Stops if user re-engages.

strengths.title: 3-email win-back campaign
strengths.detail: SendWinBackEmails command: day 3 feedback + reactivate, day 14 what's new, day 30 final gentle reminder. Deduplication via EmailSendLog.

strengths.title: D7 and D30 retention rate tracking
strengths.detail: CustomerHealthService calculates retention rates: % of users active by day 7 (created 7-10 days ago) and day 30 (created 30-33 days ago).

strengths.title: NPS survey with cooldown and eligibility
strengths.detail: NpsResponse model: 0-10 score, 90-day cooldown, eligibility checks (verified email, 7+ days old). Categories: Promoter/Passive/Detractor.

strengths.title: Public roadmap with community voting
strengths.detail: RoadmapEntry model with vote counts. Authenticated users can upvote. Status badges (Planned, In Progress, Completed). Feedback links to roadmap items.

strengths.title: Changelog with version acknowledgment
strengths.detail: ChangelogController serves JSON changelog. Tracks last-seen version per user via UserSetting. Enables 'what's new' badge.

findings.title: No cohort-based email segmentation
findings.detail: Fixed email sequences without dynamic segmentation by feature adoption, usage patterns, engagement score, or days since signup.
findings.recommendation: Add segment-based email variants that personalize content based on engagement score bands and feature adoption state.

findings.title: No gamification or achievement system
findings.detail: No badges, points, streaks, or milestone celebrations. Score visualization exists in admin but not user-facing.
findings.recommendation: Add lightweight milestone celebrations (first API call, 10th webhook delivery, etc.) as toast notifications.

findings.title: No proactive at-risk intervention
findings.detail: Lifecycle stage tracks AT_RISK but no automated intervention triggers when health score drops below threshold.
findings.recommendation: Trigger personalized outreach (email + in-app) when health score drops below 25 for 7+ days.

findings.title: No Slack notification integration for internal events
findings.detail: Events logged to audit logs but no Slack webhook for real-time team alerts on signup, churn, or payment events.
findings.recommendation: Add Slack webhook dispatch for high-value events (new paid subscription, churn, payment failure).

content_and_gtm.summary: Rich content marketing stack with blog, 9 guides, 8 competitor comparisons, 6 feature pages, changelog, and roadmap. GA4 dual-side analytics with 127+ events. UTM tracking and lead scoring. Missing A/B testing and i18n.

strengths.title: 9 long-form SEO guides covering SaaS topics
strengths.detail: GuidesController serves guides from 12-25 min read time covering Laravel SaaS, Stripe billing, feature flags, starter kit comparison, build vs buy, 2FA, webhooks, tenancy.

strengths.title: 8 competitor comparison pages
strengths.detail: CompareController: Jetstream, Spark, SaaSyKit, Wave, Shipfast, Supastarter, Larafast, Makerkit. Configurable template pricing from .env.

strengths.title: GA4 server + client analytics with 127+ events
strengths.detail: AnalyticsGateway: Measurement Protocol with PII sanitization and batch sending. Client-side: cookie consent gating, event queue, retry logic. Events synced between PHP enum and TypeScript.

strengths.title: First-touch UTM attribution pipeline
strengths.detail: CaptureUtmParameters middleware stores utm_source/medium/campaign/content/term to session. Persisted to user_settings on registration. Emails append UTM params to CTAs.

strengths.title: Product analytics admin dashboard
strengths.detail: ProductAnalyticsService: signup trend, onboarding funnel with conversion rates, activation rate, feature adoption top 10, subscription events.

strengths.title: Markdown blog with frontmatter and caching
strengths.detail: BlogController parses /resources/content/blog/*.md with YAML frontmatter. 1-hour index cache. Blog posts include BlogPosting JSON-LD.

findings.title: No A/B testing infrastructure
findings.detail: No experiment framework for testing onboarding flows, email subject lines, pricing page variants, or CTA copy. Only a single A/B pricing variant in config/plans.php.
findings.recommendation: Implement lightweight experiment assignment (user_id % N) with event tracking to measure conversion by variant.

findings.title: Only 3 blog posts published
findings.detail: Blog infrastructure is solid but content volume is minimal (3 posts). Guides (9) are more developed.
findings.recommendation: Prioritize publishing 2-4 blog posts per month targeting long-tail keywords from guide topics.

findings.title: No multi-language content support
findings.detail: All marketing content is English-only. No i18n framework integrated. formatCurrency supports locale parameter but no translation system.
findings.recommendation: Defer i18n until international expansion is planned. Current English-only is appropriate for launch.

findings.title: No multi-touch attribution
findings.detail: First-touch UTM only. No tracking of subsequent touchpoints before conversion.
findings.recommendation: Store attribution events array in user_settings for multi-touch analysis.

virality_and_referrals.summary: Weakest dimension. No referral system, no invite flow, no social sharing buttons, no affiliate program. Social auth exists for login but not for viral growth. Roadmap voting provides minimal community-driven virality.

strengths.title: Public roadmap with community voting
strengths.detail: RoadmapEntry model with authenticated voting. Creates sense of community ownership and gives users a reason to share the roadmap.

strengths.title: Shareable content pages
strengths.detail: Blog, guides, comparisons, changelog are public pages with OG tags suitable for social sharing.

strengths.title: Social auth infrastructure
strengths.detail: SocialAuthService supports Google, GitHub, Facebook, Discord. OAuth framework could be extended for share/invite flows.

findings.title: No referral or invite system
findings.detail: No referral codes, invite links, or friend-get-friend mechanics. No infrastructure for tracking referral attribution.
findings.recommendation: Implement referral system: unique referral code per user, 2-sided incentive (referrer gets credit, referee gets discount), tracking via user_settings + referral_conversions table.

findings.title: No social sharing buttons
findings.detail: Content pages have OG tags but no share buttons for Twitter, LinkedIn, or copy-link on blog, guides, or changelog.
findings.recommendation: Add lightweight share component to blog posts, guides, and comparison pages.

findings.title: No team invite flow
findings.detail: Per-seat billing exists but no mechanism for existing team members to invite colleagues. Seat addition is admin-only via quantity update.
findings.recommendation: Build team invite flow: generate invite link → email invitation → accept/join → auto-assign seat.

findings.title: No affiliate or partner program
findings.detail: No affiliate tracking, commission management, or partner portal.
findings.recommendation: Defer until post-PMF. Consider third-party affiliate platform (Rewardful, PartnerStack) over custom build.

technical_growth_infrastructure.summary: Strong security headers, rate limiting, error tracking, and health monitoring. Code splitting and lazy loading well-implemented. Missing PWA support, image optimization pipeline, and frontend error tracking.

strengths.title: Comprehensive security headers and CSP
strengths.detail: SecurityHeaders middleware: nosniff, DENY framing, strict referrer, permissions policy. Nonce-based CSP with report-only mode. HSTS with optional preload.

strengths.title: Granular rate limiting (80+ throttle configs)
strengths.detail: Per-endpoint throttling: auth 3-10/min, contact 3-5/min, API 20-120/min. Login brute-force protection. RateLimitHeaders middleware for API clients.

strengths.title: Sentry + structured logging with request tracing
strengths.detail: Sentry PHP SDK with breadcrumbs, trace sampling, profiling. RequestIdMiddleware generates UUID shared with logging + Sentry scope.

strengths.title: Multi-check health endpoint
strengths.detail: HealthCheckService: DB, cache, queue size, disk usage checks with configurable thresholds. Bearer token + IP allowlist auth. 5s cache TTL.

strengths.title: Sophisticated code splitting
strengths.detail: vite.config.ts: manual vendor chunks (React core, Radix UI, charting, icons). Lazy loading via import.meta.glob. Bundle analyzer integration.

strengths.title: Lazy loading prevention in production
strengths.detail: Model::preventLazyLoading() enforced in production. Catches N+1 queries before they reach users.

findings.title: No PWA support
findings.detail: No service worker, no web app manifest, no offline fallback, no install prompt, no push notifications. Zero PWA infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Add basic manifest.json and service worker for offline fallback page and asset caching. Defer push notifications.

findings.title: No frontend error tracking
findings.detail: VITE_SENTRY_DSN env var exists but Sentry is not initialized in React app. Frontend errors are invisible.
findings.recommendation: Initialize Sentry browser SDK in resources/js/app.tsx with error boundary integration.

findings.title: No image optimization pipeline
findings.detail: No WebP/AVIF generation, no responsive images, no image CDN. Marketing page images served as-is.
findings.recommendation: Add vite-imagetools or sharp-based build step for automatic WebP conversion and responsive variants.

findings.title: No email deliverability setup guidance
findings.detail: Multiple mailers configured (Postmark, Resend, SES) but no SPF/DKIM/DMARC documentation, no bounce handling, no complaint webhook handlers.
findings.recommendation: Document DNS records (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) in deployment guide. Add SES bounce/complaint webhook handlers if using SES.

findings.title: No automated accessibility testing
findings.detail: Radix UI provides solid a11y foundation but no jest-axe, axe-core, or pa11y testing to catch regressions.
findings.recommendation: Add jest-axe assertions to component tests for key pages (pricing, dashboard, onboarding).

expansion_and_self_serve.summary: Strong single-user billing and admin panel. Per-seat pricing configured for team tiers. Critical gap: no team/organization model, no member management, no shared workspace. Blocks B2B expansion revenue.

strengths.title: Comprehensive admin panel (20+ controllers)
strengths.detail: User management with impersonation, billing analytics, token/webhook monitoring, failed jobs, audit logs, NPS, feedback, roadmap, feature flags, health checks, data export.

strengths.title: Per-seat billing with quantity management
strengths.detail: Team tiers (pro_team: 2-4 seats, team: 2-50 seats) with updateQuantity() method. Min/max seat validation.

strengths.title: Developer platform (API tokens + webhooks)
strengths.detail: Sanctum token management with plan-based limits. Outgoing webhooks with HMAC signing, delivery tracking. Scribe API docs (feature-gated).

strengths.title: Feature flag admin with per-user overrides
strengths.detail: FeatureFlagService resolves per-user > global > config. Admin UI for runtime flag management with reason + changed_by tracking.

findings.title: No team/organization model
findings.detail: Per-seat pricing is configured but no Team/Organization model, no member management, no shared workspace, no team roles (owner/admin/member). Config/plans.php mentions 'team member management' and 'shared projects with role-based access' as features but code doesn't implement them.
findings.recommendation: Build team infrastructure: teams table, team_user pivot with role, team invitation system, team-scoped resources. This is prerequisite for B2B growth.

findings.title: No team invite or member management flow
findings.detail: Seat upgrades happen via quantity update but no mechanism for existing members to invite colleagues or manage team membership.
findings.recommendation: Build: invite link generation → email invitation → accept/join flow → auto-assign seat → team settings page.

findings.title: No usage-based or add-on revenue
findings.detail: Only flat-rate and per-seat pricing. No metered billing, overage charges, or purchasable add-ons.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate if API call metering or storage-based pricing could drive expansion. Stripe supports metered billing via subscription items.

findings.title: No SSO/SAML for enterprise tier
findings.detail: Enterprise tier lists SSO as a feature but no implementation exists. Social auth is not enterprise SSO.
findings.recommendation: Implement SAML SSO via laravel-saml2 or similar package for enterprise customers.

findings.title: No API key rotation mechanism
findings.detail: Sanctum tokens can be created and revoked but no automatic rotation, expiry warnings, or key regeneration UI.
findings.recommendation: Add token expiry display and 'regenerate' button that creates new token + revokes old one atomically.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Team features advertised but not implemented
cross_cutting_findings.detail: Plan configuration and pricing page reference team member management, shared projects, and role-based access. Database has per-seat billing. But no Team model, no member management, no shared resources exist in code. This is misleading to customers and blocks B2B expansion.
cross_cutting_findings.recommendation: Either implement team features or remove references from plan descriptions to avoid false advertising.

cross_cutting_findings.title: No referral loop to amplify paid growth
cross_cutting_findings.detail: Strong acquisition (content, SEO) and monetization but no viral coefficient. Paid users have no mechanism to bring in new users organically.
cross_cutting_findings.recommendation: Implement 2-sided referral system as highest-ROI growth investment after team features.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Analytics rich but lacks experiment framework
cross_cutting_findings.detail: 127+ events tracked with GA4 dual-side, lead scoring, health scoring, lifecycle tracking. But no way to run controlled experiments to optimize conversion.
cross_cutting_findings.recommendation: Build lightweight experiment framework using user_id hash for assignment. Track variant in analytics events.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Frontend monitoring blind spot
cross_cutting_findings.detail: Backend has Sentry + structured logging + request tracing. Frontend has VITE_SENTRY_DSN configured but not initialized. JavaScript errors invisible to operations.
cross_cutting_findings.recommendation: Initialize Sentry browser SDK in app.tsx. Add error boundary with user-friendly fallback.

priority_roadmap.title: Implement team/organization infrastructure
priority_roadmap.rationale: Per-seat billing exists but teams don't. This blocks all B2B expansion revenue and makes team tier pricing misleading.

priority_roadmap.title: Build referral system
priority_roadmap.rationale: Highest-ROI growth lever missing. Strong product but zero viral coefficient means linear growth only.

priority_roadmap.title: Abandoned checkout recovery
priority_roadmap.rationale: Direct revenue recovery with minimal effort. Listen to Stripe webhook, send email.

priority_roadmap.title: Frontend error tracking (Sentry browser SDK)
priority_roadmap.rationale: Quick win. Config exists, just needs initialization. Eliminates frontend monitoring blind spot.

priority_roadmap.title: Social sharing buttons on content pages
priority_roadmap.rationale: Quick win to amplify existing content marketing investment. OG tags already configured.

priority_roadmap.title: A/B testing framework
priority_roadmap.rationale: Rich analytics data exists but no experiment framework to optimize conversion. Needed before scaling acquisition spend.

priority_roadmap.title: LTV/CAC and churn reason analytics
priority_roadmap.rationale: Unit economics visibility needed for sustainable growth. Data already collected, needs aggregation.

priority_roadmap.title: Guided feature tours for onboarding
priority_roadmap.rationale: Improves activation rate. Health scoring infrastructure ready to measure impact.

priority_roadmap.title: PWA basic support
priority_roadmap.rationale: Manifest.json and offline fallback improve mobile experience and enable install prompt.

priority_roadmap.title: Automated accessibility testing
priority_roadmap.rationale: Radix UI provides foundation but no regression testing. Quick to add jest-axe to existing test suite.
